notes: Built-in defaults live only in DefaultWorkflow() and are applied at read time by ResolveWorkflowLayers (configresolve.go:172-183); ReadTaskFile (model/io.go) applies no defaults on unmarshal — a task file records overrides, not effective values.

WriteTaskFile round-trips presence: TestReadWriteTaskFile_RoundTrip asserts tf.Workflow==got.Workflow (line 92); TestReadWriteTaskFile_NoWorkflowKeyNormalizesToEmpty confirms a missing workflow key becomes {} with no default injection.

tp init writes an empty override; the only tf.Workflow assignment across cli is importcmd.go:127 (preservation), so no in-place writer materializes; clampWorkflowRanges only clamps at resolution and never strips a field from the file.

Storage is WorkflowOverride (pointer type distinct from resolved Workflow), so effective reads reroute through the resolver and a missed site is a compile error; go build ./... succeeds. No new command or flag added.
No --unset flag on tp set; only --bulk/--workflow/--project/--local are registered. Presence is added, not removable.

ResolveWorkflowLayers keeps precedence task-override>project>built-in unchanged; ProjectConfig schema {workflow: WorkflowOverride} is structurally unchanged. commit_strategy presence-tracking is the intended §4.1 change, not a precedence/schema restructure.
No new cobra command or flag; init/set/config flag sets unchanged and build confirms no added surface.
No automatic thinning: import preserves workflow presence; thinning happens only via config --extract (StripTaskWorkflowFields, projectconfig.go:221-251), a user-invoked step.
Built-in defaults remain a single source of truth in DefaultWorkflow(); no change to how they are defined.

commit_strategy retained: CommitStrategy field in WorkflowOverride (projectconfig.go:39) and Workflow (workflow.go:12), included in IsEmpty; tp init --commit-strategy flag kept (init.go:33,80-83) and round-tripped.

editableWorkflowFields = five integers + checks; readOnlyWorkflowFields = quality_gate, commit_strategy. Settable set and read-only status unchanged; runSetWorkflow rejects read-only fields (set.go:292-296).

CommitStrategy *string added (model/projectconfig.go:39, IsEmpty:58); parseWorkflowOverride reads commit_strategy (projectconfig.go:99-105); ResolveWorkflowLayers resolves via pickString (configresolve.go:176); TestResolveWorkflowLayers_CommitStrategy covers parse + resolution (override wins, project fallback, empty default).

TaskFile.Workflow stored as WorkflowOverride; ReadTaskFile applies no defaults (io.go); effective reads go through resolver only (plan.go:158-162, validate.go:43, gate.go:49/69 via EffectiveWorkflowForTaskFile); io_test.go round-trip matrix + NoWorkflowKeyNormalizesToEmpty; build + go test pass.

ResolveEffectiveWorkflow clamps out-of-range task override (configresolve.go:156); TestResolveEffectiveWorkflow_OutOfRangeTaskOverrideClamped (no config → 2 + 'out of range' warning) and _OutOfRangeDoesNotMaskProject (project → 3); CLI prints warnings to stderr and exits 0 (config.go:116-118).

init.go writes an empty override, setting quality_gate/commit_strategy only when flagged (62-83); TestInit_SparseWorkflowBlock covers no-flags→{}, quality-gate-only, commit-strategy-only, and both-compose, asserting no materialized defaults.

runSetWorkflow validates before any read/write and sets only the named pointer field (set.go:272-383); tests cover successful set (no siblings), out-of-range exit 1, non-parseable exit 1, non-settable exit 2, unknown exit 2 — each leaving the file unmodified.

resolvedConfig attributes source via override.X!=nil (config.go:50-79); resolveConfigWorkflow clamps override (config.go:121) via exported ClampWorkflowRanges (projectconfig.go:36-38); tests cover clamped out-of-range→source 'project' value 3 and commit_strategy→source 'override'.
spec/0.25.0.tasks.json workflow block is thinned to "workflow": {} and committed; with the repo's .tp/config.json + built-in defaults it resolves to identical effective values (verified equivalence).

resolveConfigWorkflow propagates ResolveEffectiveWorkflow error (errors.As MalformedConfigError -> exit 3); presence checks use override.X!=nil, no deref; ClampWorkflowRanges(&override) mutates a local copy not written back. commit_strategy (L57) is a nil-safe presence read.
Test file: all fixture writes use 0o600 and filepath.Join; every error asserted via require.NoError; no swallowed errors or unsafe paths.

EffectiveWorkflowForTaskFile is a lock-free read in the pre-flock section (reads are lock-free by design; tfPre already read successfully at L126); the actual write stays under engine.WithFileLock (L349). Same pattern at L341.
Test file: constructs model.Workflow directly, json.Marshal error checked via require.NoError; no file writes, no swallowed errors.
Test file: fixture writes use 0o600 + filepath.Join; os.ReadFile/json.Unmarshal errors asserted via require.NoError; no lock-free production write path exercised unsafely.

WorkflowOverride{} init is a value assignment; tf.Workflow=existing.Workflow (L127) is guarded by readErr==nil best-effort preservation (documented §9.3), existing is discarded so no aliasing hazard; target path via filepath.Join; write via model.WriteTaskFile (0o600, errors wrapped).

qg:=initQualityGate;&qg and cs:=initCommitStrategy;&cs take the address of a fresh local copy (correct pointer-to-copy pattern, no aliasing); taskFilePath via filepath.Join; WriteTaskFile writes 0o600; runEjectRoles uses filepath.Join + os.WriteFile 0o600 and propagates every error.
Test file: fixture writes 0o600 + filepath.Join; os.ReadFile/json.Unmarshal errors checked via require.NoError; no swallowed errors.

override:=tf.Workflow is a struct copy; ClampWorkflowRanges reassigns the copy's pointer fields to nil (*p=nil) without mutating shared underlying ints or tf.Workflow (verified projectconfig.go L17-31); ResolveWorkflowLayers pickers are nil-safe (configresolve.go L105-132). No aliasing corruption, no nil-deref.
Test file: fixture write 0o600 + filepath.Join; json.Unmarshal error via require.NoError; no swallowed errors or unsafe paths.

resolvedConfig/resolveConfigWorkflow both have doc comments; commit_strategy row added cleanly, dmap uses make(map,len); ClampWorkflowRanges call documented (§3.4). No %w gap (errors.As+output.Error is a terminal handler). No nil-slice-to-JSON, no TODO/dead code.
Two well-named tests, each with a doc comment tying to §3.4/§10.7/§10.8. Clear assertions, no dead code, naming follows project test conventions.

Both sites reroute the effective read to engine.EffectiveWorkflowForTaskFile per §4.2 (no reading effective values off storage). Change is 2 lines; the pre-existing helper lengths (runDoneSingle/Multi/Batch) are untouched by this release and out of scope.
gateFailureMessage tests use model.Workflow literals with clear names and JSONEq null-serialization check. No maintainability or convention issues.
readPersistedWorkflow helper marks t.Helper(); subtests are self-contained and well-named; comments reference §3.6/§10.11 correctly. No dead code, no nil-slice-to-JSON.

Bare-array branch now seeds Workflow: model.WorkflowOverride{} (§3.6), consistent with init.go. Naming/idiom correct. The pre-existing §9.3 cross-ref comment at L121 predates this release and was not touched by the +1/-1 diff.

runInit stays 54 lines. Sparse WorkflowOverride{} shell; quality_gate/commit_strategy set via the standard take-address-of-local-copy pointer idiom (qg:=...; &qg). Coverage/Tasks use make-equivalent literals ([]string{}, []model.Task{}). No nil-slice-to-JSON, no TODO, no dead code.
Table-driven test with doc comment covering §10.1; cases cover no-flags/quality-gate/commit-strategy/both. Clean naming, no dead code.

override clamp + ResolveWorkflowLayers(ProjectWorkflowOverride)) reads through the resolution path per §3.5, matching config/done. Exported engine helpers are documented. Discarding the clamp []string warnings mirrors EffectiveWorkflowForTaskFile and is a correctness/contract concern, not maintainability. Pre-existing runPlan length is untouched and out of scope.
Single focused test with doc comment (§3.5/§7.1 consistency), external cli_test package, clear assertion on clamped float64(2). No convention issues.

resolvedConfig builds a fixed-key map[string]any (json.Marshal sorts keys -> deterministic); checks value = resolved Workflow.Checks which is always non-nil [] via pickChecks/DefaultWorkflow, never null. runConfig documents --compact as a no-op for the non-task shape; malformed config -> ExitFile(3) with repair/delete hint. No missing/renamed keys.

Asserts source-label contract: a clamped out-of-range override reports source 'project' with the resolved value (not 'override'/verbatim), and a task-file commit_strategy reports source 'override'. Correctly locks the {value,source} attribution shape.

Output maps use fixed keys; closedIDs/failed init as make([],0) so empties serialize [] not null; exit codes 2/4/1 correct; closure error carries actionable hint. v0.26.0 reroute reads gate via EffectiveWorkflowForTaskFile (resolution path) not the storage struct. Result shape unchanged.
Verifies timeout serializes exit_code as null and message reports seconds; non-zero exit names the gate cmd. Runtime confirmed: gate failure messages are actionable and JSON-shape stable.

Asserts presence-preserving import contract: new bare-array import persists an empty workflow block (no materialized convergence/timeout fields), and workflow presence is preserved/overridden per source. Locks the {} container and no-default-materialization output.

Bare-array wrap sets Workflow: model.WorkflowOverride{} -> serializes as workflow:{} (object, not null); workflow-preservation copies existing.Workflow (both override type). Output {imported,path} deterministic; exit codes 2(usage)/3(file)/1(validation) correct with actionable hints.

init writes model.WorkflowOverride{} -> workflow:{}; only sets QualityGate/CommitStrategy pointers when flags are non-empty, so the block stays sparse. Runtime confirmed init shell emits {}. Exit 2/3 with hints; eject error 'use --force to overwrite' actionable.

Table test asserts tf[workflow] equals exactly the flagged fields, including the empty {} for the no-flags case. Directly locks §4.4/§10.1: object container, no materialized defaults, both flags compose.

planResult.Workflow is *Workflow with omitempty (--minimal omits it); non-minimal path resolves via ResolveWorkflowLayers over a clamped copy (reroute off storage struct, no aliasing of tf.Workflow). Resolved checks is [] not null. execution_order slices use make; all-done -> exit 4; deterministic struct-order JSON.

Asserts tp plan stdout workflow.review_clean_rounds is the clamped effective value (2), matching config/done/review, so an out-of-range authored value is never echoed verbatim in agent-facing output. Runtime and unit test both pass.
